scope_note: QA Langlands GL4 Induction — AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f) Euler Factors cert [404]. Primary sources: Arthur, J. and Clozel, L. (1989) 'Simple Algebras, Base Change, and the Advanced Theory of the Trace Formula' Annals of Mathematics Studies 120, Princeton University Press ISBN 978-0-691-08517-3 (automorphic induction for GL_n); Langlands, R.P. (1980) 'Base Change for GL(2)' Annals of Mathematics Studies 96, Princeton University Press ISBN 978-0-691-08258-5 (base change functoriality for GL_2); Weil, A. (1967) 'Basic Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-90420-7 (local L-factors, Euler products); LMFDB Collaboration (2024) doi:10.1007/978-3-031-33460-0 (eigenvalue data). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f = 2.2.5.1-125.1-a (GL_2/Q(sqrt5), CM by Q(zeta_5)), the GL_4/Q automorphic induction AI_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(f) has Euler polynomial at each split prime p (p≡1 mod 5): P_p(Y) = (1-a_𝔭*Y+p*Y^2)(1-sigma_F(a_𝔭)*Y+p*Y^2) = 1-T*Y+(N+2p)*Y^2-p*T*Y^3+p^2*Y^4 where T=Tr_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a_p)=2u+v and N=N_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a_p)=u^2+u*v-v^2. Four checks: (C1) T ≡ -1 ≡ 4 mod 5 for all 22 split primes p<=500 (conductor arithmetic: Frobenius pi≡1 mod lambda^3 forces Tr_{K/Q}(pi)≡4 mod 5 since sum of 4 units each ≡1 mod prime above 5); (C2) ℤ[phi]-factorization verified: product (1-a_𝔭*Y+pY^2)(1-sigma_F(a_𝔭)*Y+pY^2) over Z[phi][Y] gives integer coefficients 1,-T,N+2p,-pT,p^2 for all 22 primes; (C3) palindrome/functional-equation purity: P_p(Y) = p^2*Y^4*P_p(1/(p*Y)), equivalently a_4=p^2*a_0 and a_3=p*a_1; (C4) GL4 Ramanujan: all 4 roots of P_p(Y) have |root|=p^{-1/2}, inherited from [403] Ramanujan equality. Langlands ladder: [403] GL2/Q(sqrt5) Frobenius+Pell -> [404] THIS GL4/Q Euler factors. Theorem NT: all arithmetic in Z[phi] (integers); float embeddings are observer projections for |root| verification only. Derived 2026-06-13.
